content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA

EDMUND G. BROWN JR., Governor

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
3 "L" STREET
SACRAMENTO 95814

May 28, 1975

A-75-198

Mr. A. Robert Throckmorton
Munns, Kofford, Holt & Hunt
Hunt & Throckmorton
Attorneys at Law
Suite 300, Pasadena Federal Building
199 North Lake
Pasadena, California 91101

Re: Request for Opinion
    No. 75-122

Dear Mr. Throckmorton:

     Thank you for your letter of May 13, 1975,
requesting an opinion with respect to your duties under
the Political Reform Act.

     Since your request raises no substantial ques-
tion involving interpretation of the Political Reform
Act, your request for a formal opinion is denied. Out-
going members of a Board of Appeals of a city are not
presently required to file statements under the Political
Reform Act. Such agencies will be required to adopt con-
flict of interest codes during 1976, but no disclosure
requirements are applicable until such a code is adopted.

     Although for the reasons stated no formal opinion
will be issued, I hope the foregoing is sufficiently help-
ful. If you have any further questions, I urge you to
contact a member of the Commission staff.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        Daniel H. Lowenstein
                                        Chairman, for the
                                        Commission

DHL:cjb

. . . . . .

Request No. 75 3.2%
Date Received 5-19-75
Response Due 6-2-75

Messrs. Korford, Hoffman, Hunt & Throckmorton
ATTORNEYS AT LAW
SUITE 350, PASADENA FEDERAL BUILDING
193 NORTH LAS
PASADENA, CALIFORNIA 91101

GREG JOFFORD
GAVIN J MURNS
RALPH M. HOFFMAN
GEORGE HUNT
SPENCER S ROBINSON
A. ROBERT THROCKMORTON
DAVID G. WATKINS
PHILIP C. JONDICE
JERRY S CAHAN
DENNIS P. MATTHEWS
PAUL H. JOHNSON

Glen Bentner
(836-1963

Of Counsel:
C. E. M. MCDONNELL

AREA CODE 213
MURRAY 1-2331
SYCAMORE 5-5733

May 13, 1975

The Fair Political Practice Commission
926 J Street, Suite 255
Sacramento, California 95814

RE: Statement of Economic Interest--
Form 720 "Disclosure of Financial
Interest Under Preposition 9"

Gentlemen:

Please be advised that during the past year and
a half I have been a member of the Board of Appeals for the
city of Monrovia. In May, 1975, our Board was terminated
by the City Council. It has been suggested that the Board
members must file Form 720 within 30 days. As a member of
the State Bar of California and as one engaged in private
practice, I find that the conflict exists with regard to
interim form schedule 720-F which requires that I, "List and
name every person who paid fees to the business entity if
your prorata share of the fees was $1,000.00 or more." It
is my understanding that Government Code Section 83114 pro-
vides for advisory opinions. May I, therefore, solicite an
advisory opinion as to the following points:

1. Whether or not a member of the Board of Appeals
of the city of Monrovia, a non-elective position, is required
to file the Disclosure Statement pursuant to Section 87202 of
the Government Code.

2. Whether or not a procedure has been developed
and, if so, please outline said procedure, for the claiming
of privilege as to the disclosure of information regarding
attorneys' clients.

Since time is of the essence, I would appreciate
your earliest possible attention to this matter. A positive
position has not been taken with regard to this matter, per-
haps an extension of time would be appropriate and I would,
therefore, request said time extension.

Very truly yours,

MUNNS, KORFORD, HOFFMAN,
HUNT & THROCKMORTON

[signature]
A. Robert Throckmorton

ART:sg
